name: Filter Bar
description: A search and filter toolbar for list pages. Combines text search with sorting, filtering, and optional tabs for quick category switching. This is the primary control bar for data tables and list views.
whenToUse: Above data tables or list views
When users need to search, sort, and filter records
In case management, client lists, or any searchable data view

components.name: GoabTabs
components.purpose: Quick category switching (e.g., All, To Do, In Progress)

components.name: GoabFormItem
components.purpose: Wraps search input with error handling

components.name: GoabFilterChip
components.purpose: Display active filters with dismiss capability

structure.row2: Search input + Search button | Sort dropdown + Filter button
structure.row3: Filter chips row (when filters are active)

example.description: Complete filter bar with tabs, search, sort, and chips
example.code:
{/* Tabs for quick filtering */} {/* Search and filter row */}
setInputValue(e.value)} onKeyPress={(e) => e.key === 'Enter' && applyFilter()} /> Search
setSortField(e.value)} placeholder="Sort by" size="compact" width="160px" > setFilterDrawerOpen(true)} > Filter
{/* Filter chips row - shown when filters are active */} {activeFilters.length > 0 && (
{activeFilters.map((chip) => ( removeFilter(chip)} /> ))} Clear all
)}

variants.name: Simple (search only)
variants.description: Just search input and button, no tabs or advanced filters

variants.name: With tabs
variants.description: Add GoabTabs above search for quick category switching

variants.name: With filter drawer
variants.description: Filter button opens GoabDrawer with checkbox filters by category

variants.name: Search in header
variants.description: Move search to PageHeader actions for a cleaner layout

considerations: Use compact size for all components in filter bars
Handle Enter key on search input for keyboard users
Show clear error messages for empty or duplicate searches
Filter chips should always have a 'Clear all' option
Consider mobile layout - controls should wrap gracefully
